data.title: US Natural Gas Stocks Posts Larger-Gain Than Expected in Week Ended March 28
data.description:
US natural gas stocks rose by 29 billion cubic feet in the week ended March 28, higher than the 27 billion increase expected in a survey compiled by Bloomberg as of 7:40 am ET and following an increase of 37 billion cubic feet in the previous week.

Stocks at 1.773 trillion cubic feet are 21.7% lower than in the comparable week a year ago and 4.3% below their 5-year average.

data.title: Lucid Delivers a Q1 Surprise But Stock Struggles to Recover YTD Losses
data.description:
Lucid Group reported first-quarter deliveries of 3,109 vehicles, surpassing analysts' expectations of 2,947. The company said it produced 2,212 vehicles during the quarter that ended March 31 and noted that more than 600 additional vehicles were in transit to Saudi Arabia for final assembly.

The delivery total not only exceeded projections but also showed strong year-over-year improvement. Lucid delivered 1,927 vehicles in the same quarter last year and 3,099 units in the fourth quarter of 2024. For the full year 2024, Lucid delivered 10,241 vehicles a 70% increase compared to 2023.

The electric vehicle manufacturer plans to release its Q1 earnings report on May 6.

Despite the upbeat delivery numbers, shares of Lucid were down more than 3.5% in premarket trading Thursday. It is important to note that the stock has traded between $1.93 and $4.43 over the past year. Moreover, Lucid Group's short interest is currently 284,950,000 shares, marking a 6.23% increase from the previous month. This represents 23.95% of the company's 1,190,000,000 share float.

Over the past week, the stock notched a 1.91% gain, outpacing the S&P 500's 0.72% decline. For the month, Lucid is up 8.11%, while the broader market fell 4.76%. However, over the past six months, the stock has dropped 28.14% and remains down 20.53% year to date. By contrast, the S&P 500 has slipped just 3.58% since the start of the year.

data.title: US Natural Gas Storage Rises More than Expected
data.description:
US utilities added 29 billion cubic feet of gas to storage during the week ending March 28, 2025, exceeding market expectations of a 27 billion cubic feet build.

This marked the third consecutive week of increases in US natural gas stocks.

The biggest rise was seen in the South Central region (+33bcf), followed by the Pacific (+10bcf), and the Mountain (+2bcf).

Storage levels are now 21.7% lower than the same period last year and 4.3% below the five-year average.

data.title: Spain's Iberdrola surpasses 100 billion euro market capitalisation mark
data.description:
Spanish utility company Iberdrola surpassed the 100 billion euro ($110.90 billion) market capitalisation mark for the first time on Thursday, according to LSEG data.

Shares of Europe's largest utility were up 2.9% to 15.64 euros at 1432 GMT, on track for their best day in more than two years pushing the company's market value to 100.12 billion euros.

($1 = 0.9017 euros)

data.title: Slump in Canadian Exports Seen The Start of Stretch of WeaknessMarket Talk
data.description: The tariff front-running boost to Canadian exports reversed sharply in February, causing the country's trade balance to swing to a C$1.5 billion deficit for the month, CIBC Capital Markets' Katherine Judge says. The result was sharply below the consensus expectation for a C$3.5 billion surplus. The economist anticipates exports will remain under pressure, since while USMCA-tied goods were exempt from tariffs introduced in March businesses in the U.S. appear to have accumulated enough inventory in prior months. Judge says export demand will also be dented for autos, steel and aluminum, and lumber, which are subject to previously announced tariffs, despite Canada escaping the latest round of so-called reciprocal U.S. tariffs. And, she adds, a higher effective tariff rate on U.S. imports as a whole will work to slow U.S. and global growth and will weigh on export demand for Canada ahead. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: New Circana Snacking Research Reveals How Health, Flavor, and Innovation Are Redefining America's Cravings
data.description:
Chicago, April 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Circana, LLC unveiled its latest research, Snack Unwrap: The Insatiable Craving for Growth, offering valuable insights into emerging trends shaping U.S. consumers’ snacking habits. This comprehensive research reveals how evolving consumer priorities, a growing focus on health-conscious choices, and broader macroeconomic factors are transforming snacking behaviors. The report also emphasizes the essential role of innovation in fostering growth within the fast-paced and highly competitive snacking industry.

"Snacking continues to play a vital role in consumers' lives, meeting needs ranging from quick hunger fixes to indulgent cravings and wellness goals," said Sally Lyons Wyatt, global executive vice president and chief industry advisor for Circana. "While snacks have driven dollar sales growth for years, unit sales tell a more nuanced story, with declines in 2022 and 2023, a softened decline in 2024, and a renewed dip as 2025 begins. This underscores how consumer snacking habits are evolving. Brands have a significant opportunity to adapt by leveraging health trends, personalization, and innovation to align with these shifting preferences and expectations."

Key insights from the 2025 report include:

  • Snacking Frequency Remains High: Despite economic uncertainties, snacking remains an integral part of daily life for U.S. consumers. Nearly half of Americans (48.8%) snack three or more times a day, a 2.7% increase year-over-year, with younger demographics (ages 18-44) leading the charge.
  • Healthier Options on the Rise: Consumers are increasingly seeking snacks that align with their dietary and wellness goals. Approximately 64.1% actively look for snacks perceived as "good for them," marking a significant 7.4% increase since 2020. Categories like yogurt, natural cheese chunks, and high-protein options are experiencing robust growth due to their nutritional appeal.
  • Macroeconomic Influences Drive Value-Oriented Choices: Rising concerns about inflation and cost have led to more strategic purchasing practices. Consumers are still turning to multipack and variety options. In addition, sales growth of private-label snacks is outpacing branded products in several core categories.
  • Innovation Fuels Consumer Demand: From functional snacks with health benefits to indulgent treats with nostalgic twists, brands are successfully appealing to diverse consumption needs. Viral flavor profiles such as ube, peri peri, and matcha are fostering curiosity, while collaborations between brands are creating buzz-worthy products.
  • Emerging Channels and Trends: Online sales of snacks continue to climb, with nearly 50% of consumers stating they are purchasing snacks online in 2024. Convenience, flexible delivery options, and price-surfing behaviors have all contributed to the growing prominence of e-commerce in the snacking sector.

Lyons Wyatt added, “Snacking has evolved beyond simply satisfying hunger; it has become less about impulse and more of a reflection of personal values, priorities, and lifestyle choices. From the rise of health-forward products to the experimental exploration of bold flavors, brands must adapt quickly to meet consumer needs and keep pace with shifting trends.”

Learn more about Snack Unwrap: The Insatiable Craving for Growth research and discover how snacking trends are transforming the CPG industry.

About Circana

Circana is a leader in providing technology, AI, and data to fast-moving consumer packaged goods companies, durables manufacturers, and retailers seeking to optimize their businesses. Circana’s predictive analytics and technology empower clients to measure their market share, understand the underlying consumer behavior driving it, and accelerate their growth. Circana’s Liquid Data® technology platform is powered by an expansive, high-quality data set and intelligent algorithms trained on six decades of domain expertise. With Circana, clients can take immediate action to future-proof and evolve their growth strategies amid an increasingly complex, fast-paced, and ever-changing economy. Learn more at circana.com.

Shelley Hughes

Circana

+1 312-731-1782

Shelley.Hughes@circana.com

data.title: Netstreit Is Maintained at Outperform by Mizuho
Berenberg Sees Value and Indexed Property Income Trust's REIT Transition Boosting Investor Base
data.description:
Berenberg on Wednesday maintained Value and Indexed Property Income Trust's buy rating and price target of 2.15 pounds sterling, saying it expects the company's recent transition into a UK real estate investment trust to further expand its investor base over time.

As of March 31, the company said its portfolio amounted to 146 million pounds, with 79% of its annual rent coming from its top 10 tenants including British retailers Marks and Spencer Group and J Sainsbury . The company also made several property disposals during the fiscal year that were above book value and added a garden center to its portfolio.

"While VIP only selectively sells non-core properties, we see the realised sales prices as an indication that the assets are marketable and the underlying portfolio valuation reasonable," analysts noted.

data.title: Crypto Markets Wipe Out $150B After Trump's Tariff Shock Rocks Global Trade
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and broader crypto markets swung wildly Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump declared a national emergency and introduced sweeping tariffs on 180 countries, including major trade partners China, Vietnam, and Taiwan.

The surprise announcement, made during a Rose Garden address, jolted financial markets, sending Bitcoin surging to $88,500 before sharply falling back to $82,876. Ethereum dropped over 6% to $1,797, and the overall crypto market lost $150 billion in a matter of hours, according to CoinGecko.

Trump's new Made in America tariffs are among the most aggressive in decades. China was hit with a 34% levy, while Vietnam once seen as a safer manufacturing base received a 46% rate, even higher than Beijing. Taiwan, facing a 32% tariff, called the move highly unreasonable. The broader plan aims to revive U.S. manufacturing but has rattled investors and trade partners alike.

Initially, traders thought the announcement might provide clarity and spark a rally. Rachael Lucas of BTC Markets said the spike was fueled by uncertainty relief and larger players seizing the chance to cash out. But that optimism evaporated quickly. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index plunged to 25, reflecting extreme fear.

Despite the downturn, some industry voices suggested a potential upside. David Hernandez of 21Shares told Cointelegraph the tariff shock could push more institutional players into crypto as a hedge. The announcement provided much-needed clarity institutional investors may see an opportunity, he said.

Still, volatility remains high. As the full scope of the tariffs sinks in, especially with key countries like China and Vietnam blindsided, market participants across sectors are bracing for further disruption. For crypto, the next move may depend less on fundamentals and more on geopolitics.

data.title: Luxtoday.lu Releases a Complimentary Luxembourg Tax Calculator for 2025
data.description:
Luxtoday.lu updates its Luxembourg tax calculator for 2025 - a helpful complimentary tool for residents and expats in Luxembourg

Luxembourg, Luxembourg--(Newsfile Corp. - April 3, 2025) - Luxembourg's tax regulations change annually, and to help residents and expats navigate the latest updates, Luxtoday.lu has released an updated version of its tax calculator, now reflecting 2025 data.

Luxtoday.lu tax calculator

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8871/246158_231c64fdc4729bfc_001.jpg

This tool allows users to quickly estimate the amount of tax they will need to pay based on their income level, tax class, and family status. With this update, individuals can better plan their finances and avoid unexpected tax burdens.

One of the most popular tools on Luxtoday.lu

Since its launch, the tax calculator has become one of the most visited pages on the company's website. The number of users continues to grow, with the highest traffic coming from the English-language version, showing strong interest from international professionals moving to Luxembourg.

Among the most active users of the calculator are:

  • Expats and international workers planning their relocation and looking to estimate their tax burden
  • Freelancers and entrepreneurs who need to understand their tax obligations
  • Salaried employees who want to plan their finances efficiently
  • Families and married couples exploring tax classes and potential deductions

The service is available in four languages: English, French, German, and Russian, making it accessible to a diverse audience.

The latest version of the tax calculator includes:

  • The most up-to-date tax rates and income brackets
  • Updates on tax classes and deductions
  • An improved interface for a smoother user experience
  • Expanded language support for an international audience

Helping people make informed financial decisions

Luxtoday.lu aims to provide not just a convenient tool, but also to enhance financial literacy among Luxembourg's residents and expats. Understanding tax obligations allows people to:

  • Plan their annual budget more effectively
  • Make informed decisions about savings and investments
  • Assess potential tax deductions and refunds

Try the updated Luxembourg tax calculator now

Luxtoday.lu continues to develop services that simplify life in Luxembourg. The updated 2025 tax calculator is now live.

Try the Tax Calculator here

For any questions or suggestions, feel free to contact Luxtoday.lu at editorial@luxtoday.lu.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/246158

data.title: Puma: Appointment of Baeumer Has Been Effective Since Apr. 1
Pharma Stocks Aren't in the Clear, Despite Tariff Exemption-Heard on the Street
data.description:
By David Wainer

Big pharmaceutical stocks mostly rose after President Trump's executive order exempted medicines from new tariffs. Merck, Bristol-Myers Squibb, and Johnson & Johnson were among the top performers in the S&P 500, with the sector also benefiting from its status as a defensive play.

Still, drugmakers aren't in the clear. Trump singled them out in his remarks Wednesday, saying: "The pharmaceutical companies are going to come roaring back... because if they don't, they got a big tax to pay."

That underscores Trump's "continued appetite to effect change in the industry," said UBS analyst Trung Huynh. One possible scenario is Trump introduces tariffs specifically targeting pharma-either all at once, or in stages-said Huynh. The president could also pursue tax reform aimed at the industry's offshore tax shelters.

For now, pharma is breathing a sigh of relief. But the reprieve may be temporary.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: EIA: US Total Working Gas In Storage +29 Bcf At 1773 Bcf
Cocoa Soars, Coffee and Sugar Slide After U.S. TariffsMarket Talk
data.description: Cocoa and coffee prices are mixed in afternoon trade after U.S. President Trump rattled global markets with harsher-than-expected tariffs on major trading partners, including key commodity producers. Vietnam, Indonesia and Brazil--all major growers of robusta coffee--were targeted with tariffs of 46%, 32% and 10%, respectively. Top cocoa growers Ivory Coast and Ghana face 21% and 10% levies. "Many commodities trade flows from the most affected producers will now enter a labyrinth in order to find demand in other countries at an efficiency cost," says Rabobank's Carlos Mera. "Coffee and chocolate will become more expensive in the U.S." New York-traded cocoa futures soar 4.3% to $9,357 a ton, likely boosted by U.S. dollar weakness, while coffee is down 2.2% to $3.80 a pound. Sugar prices fall 2.5% to 19 cents a pound. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Ericsson and Lenovo settle patent litigation
data.description:
Ericsson
  • Settlement partially resolves patent licensing dispute between the parties, with financial impacts expected to be recognized from Q2 2025
  • The parties further agree on arbitration to fully and finally resolve the remaining patent licensing dispute between the parties
  • Settlement ends all ongoing patent-related legal proceedings between the parties

Ericsson and Lenovo have reached a settlement to resolve their patent licensing dispute over a multi-year, global patent cross-license agreement between the two companies.

As part of the settlement, all ongoing lawsuits and administrative proceedings filed by both companies in several countries, including the actions pending before the United States International Trade Commission (USITC), will be withdrawn.

Financial impacts are expected to be recognized from Q2 2025, reflecting the partial settlement. The remaining patent licensing dispute will be fully resolved through an arbitration process between the parties.

Ericsson is a leading contributor to 3GPP and to the development of global mobile standards for the benefit of consumers and enterprises everywhere. The value of Ericsson’s patent portfolio of more than 60,000 granted patents is strengthened by our leading position as a 5G vendor, and annual investments of more than SEK 50 b in R&D. The company is confident of growing its IPR revenues with new 5G agreements and by expanding into other licensing areas long term.

NOTES TO EDITORS:

We offer mobile communication and connectivity solutions for service providers and enterprises. Together with our customers and partners, we make the digital world of tomorrow a reality. www.ericsson.com

data.title: Stirista, LLC and Skydeo, Inc. Resolve Trademark Dispute Through Consent Judgment
data.description:
San Antonio, April 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Stirista, LLC (“Stirista”), a leader in data-driven marketing solutions, and Skydeo, Inc. (“Skydeo”) have resolved a legal dispute involving allegations of trademark infringement and related claims with the entry of a Consent Judgment in the United States District Court for the District of Delaware.

The lawsuit, filed by Stirista against Skydeo, centered around the unauthorized use of Stirista’s trademarked 123Push™ mark. Stirista contended that Skydeo had utilized the 123Push™ trademark on Skydeo’s website without permission and in violation of Stirista’s intellectual property rights. Stirista also claimed that Skydeo had used the 123Push™ trademark without Stirista’s authorization to sell, offer for sale, and broker sales of products.

Weeks before the trial was scheduled to occur, Skydeo agreed to resolve the matter through a Consent Judgment. As part of the judgment, Skydeo has agreed to cease any use of the 123Push™trademark and to refrain from using the trademark in any form, including on websites, in any marketing or advertising, or search engine optimization.

The Consent Judgment includes the following key terms:

  • Skydeo must discontinue the use of the 123Push™ or Stirista™ trademarks on its website and other platforms, including search engine optimization and marketing efforts.
  • Skydeo and its affiliated parties are PERMANENTLY PROHIBITED from selling, offering for sale, or distributing any products under the 123Push™ trademark or any of Stirista’s trademarks. Skydeo cannot associate themselves with Stirista’s 123Push™ audiences or products.

The court also confirmed that it has retained jurisdiction over the matter to enforce the terms of the Consent Judgment and the confidential settlement agreement.

“Stirista is pleased to have reached a resolution with Skydeo, ensuring the protection of our intellectual property and upholding the integrity of our data products and brands,” 

said Steve Rinaldi, CFO of Stirista. “We remain committed to protecting our trademarks and will take all necessary steps to prevent infringement and market confusion.”

Stirista continues to be the owner of U.S. Trademark Registrations for the 123Push™ and Stirista™ trademarks, and the company remains dedicated to advancing its marketing solutions and protecting its brand and products from unauthorized use.

For more information about Stirista and its services, please visit https://www.stirista.com/

Legal & Court Documents:

Stirista vs. Skydeo Complaint 

Stirista vs. Skydeo Consent Judgement

About Stirista

Stirista is a data-driven marketing technology provider that combines the power of authoritative identity data with the execution of omnichannel marketing. Through its data and customer-centric approach, Stirista is helping Fortune 500 and mid-market brands increase brand loyalty and acquire new customers. Stirista's privacy compliant data insights helps clients interact with customers and prospects via digital, email and social channels. For more information, please visit http://www.stirista.com. 

Contact Information:

Gene Cyranski

Stirista

312-952-0181

data.title: US retail and luxury stocks tumble after new Trump tariffs
data.description:
** Shares of major U.S. retailers are down after U.S. President Donald Trump imposes a raft of new reciprocal tariffs on Asian production hubs

** Analysts expect this to increase costs of production for retailers at a time when consumers are becoming cautious on spending amid growing economic uncertainty

** Big-box retailer Target shares fall 11.2% to a five-year low of $93.89

** Shares of retail giant Walmart down 2% and e-commerce giant Amazon.com down 7.7%

** Electronics retailer Best Buy down 16% and membership-only retail chain Costco falls nearly 1%

** Discount store Dollar Tree falls 10.5%

** Apparel makers PVH Corp down 11.7%, American Eagle Outfitters falls 16.6%, Abercrombie & Fitch down 14.6% and Gap slips 21.7%

** Luxury firms Ralph Lauren down 17.2%, Tapestry falls 13.8%; Capri Holdings down 22.3%, hitting over 4-1/2-year low of $15.06

** Up to last close, S&P 500 Consumer Discretionary Distribution & Retail Index (.SPXRT) down more than 7% YTD

data.title: US Natural Gas Stocks Rose by 29 Bln Cubic Feet in Week Ended March 28 Vs. Expected 27 Bln Increase; Prior Gain of 37 Bln
Puma says Arthur Hoeld to become new chief executive
data.description: German sportswear group Puma has named Arthur Hoeld to be the company's new chief executive, the company said on Thursday.

data.title: Equitable Holdings Reports Final Results of Tender Offer for AllianceBernstein Holding Units
data.description:
Equitable Holdings reported the final results of its cash tender offer to buy up to 46 million units of AllianceBernstein Holding at $38.50 apiece for up to $1.8 billion.

About 19.7 million units were tendered under the offer, which expired on Tuesday, with payment totaling $757.8 million, Equitable said Thursday in a statement.

The units accepted for purchase represent almost 18% of the outstanding units as of Dec. 31, leaving Equitable with a 68.6% stake in AllianceBernstein LP, the operating partnership of AllianceBernstein Holding.

Equitable shares fell 9.3%, and AllianceBernstein Holding dropped 4.3% in recent Thursday trading as the broader equity market tumbled.

data.title: Staff Direct Launches New AI-Powered Platform to Enhance Recruitment Efficiency
data.description:
London, United Kingdom--(Newsfile Corp. - April 3, 2025) - Staff Direct, a UK-based recruitment agency specialising in temporary and permanent staffing solutions, has announced the launch of a new artificial intelligence (AI) platform designed to streamline the hiring process for employers and candidates.

Team Staff Direct

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/9032/247223_53668ead749b9b2b_001full.jpg

The platform is part of a broader investment by Staff Direct to modernise its core recruitment operations and provide faster, data-driven candidate matching. The system uses advanced algorithms to assess candidate suitability based on skills, experience, availability, and role requirements-reducing time-to-hire and improving overall efficiency.

"This development marks a significant step forward in how we deliver staffing services," said James Patel, Head of Operations at Staff Direct. "The integration of AI allows us to respond more quickly to client needs, especially in sectors where time-sensitive placements are critical."

In construction staffing, Staff Direct's new platform ensures that employers have access to skilled labourers and specialists who meet strict compliance and certification requirements. For projects operating under tight deadlines, the system facilitates rapid placement of workers with relevant expertise, such as site managers, electricians, or carpenters, enhancing project efficiency and minimizing downtime.

Similarly, the platform optimises warehouse staffing by assisting organisations in filling positions for pickers, packers, forklift operators, and supervisors. Through its real-time tracking and candidate-matching capabilities, businesses can secure reliable workers for peak operational periods, maintaining productivity even during sudden surges in demand.

The new system is being rolled out across key sectors served by Staff Direct, including hospitality, healthcare, logistics, administration, and IT. The platform supports both short-term and long-term recruitment needs, offering businesses the ability to secure suitable candidates within hours, rather than days.

Staff Direct reports that early implementation has already resulted in measurable improvements in placement speed and candidate engagement.

"We're continuing to refine the technology based on feedback from our internal recruitment teams and clients," Patel added. "Our aim is to ensure that technology enhances-not replaces-the human judgment and personalised service our clients expect."

Alongside the launch, Staff Direct will maintain its existing support services, including payroll management and compliance solutions, to provide a full-service offering for employers. This initiative aligns with Staff Direct's ongoing strategy to adopt technology that increases operational effectiveness while maintaining a focus on relationship-driven recruitment.

About Staff Direct

Staff Direct is a UK-based staffing agency providing temporary, contract, and permanent recruitment services across a wide range of industries. With a focus on combining technology with human expertise, Staff Direct aims to deliver responsive, efficient hiring solutions tailored to the needs of modern businesses.

Media Contact:

Team Staff Direct

Email: Team@Staff-Direct.co.uk

Phone: 020 3627 5550

Website: https://Staff-Direct.co.uk

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/247223

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service and must also be emailed to the Takeover Panel at monitoring@disclosure.org.uk.  The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's dealing disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.
